title: Add Sign-Up Options & Interactive Anime Character Mascot with Plant Info Board

requirements.summary: Add sign-up/login screen with Gmail or mobile number authentication before main app access
acceptanceCriteria: A dedicated sign-up/login screen is presented to new users before accessing the app.
User can enter a Gmail address or a mobile phone number to register.
Entered identifier is validated (basic email format check or phone number format check).
User profile is saved to the backend with the Gmail or mobile number identifier.
Returning users can log back in with the same identifier.
After successful sign-up/login, the user is taken to the main app (WelcomeScreen or camera view).

file_operations.description: Create a new SignUpScreen component that renders before WelcomeScreen. Display input fields for either Gmail address or mobile phone number with toggle/tab selection. Validate email format (basic regex) and phone number format (digits only). Use the authorization component's useInternetIdentity hook for authentication state. After Internet Identity login, call saveCallerUserProfile with the entered identifier (gmail or phone variant). On successful profile save, transition to WelcomeScreen. Show loading states during save and clear error messages. Style using Tailwind with the app's forest-green theme tokens.

Modify App.tsx to add a new 'signUp' view state. Use the authorization component to check if the user is authenticated and has a profile. If authenticated but no profile exists (getCallerUserProfile returns null), render SignUpScreen. If profile exists, proceed to WelcomeScreen or camera as before. Import and integrate SignUpScreen component. Ensure the sign-up screen appears before any other app content for new users. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Add a new React Query mutation hook useSaveUserProfile that wraps actor.saveCallerUserProfile. Include invalidation of the 'currentUserProfile' query key on success so the app immediately reflects the new profile. Export the hook for use in SignUpScreen. Follow existing query hook patterns in the file.

requirements.summary: Add draggable anime character mascot overlay on CameraView screen
acceptanceCriteria: An anime character illustration is rendered as an overlay on the CameraView component.
The character is visible as soon as the camera view opens.
The character can be dragged and repositioned anywhere on the screen via touch (mobile) and mouse (desktop).
The character remains within screen bounds and does not disappear off-screen.
The character does not block camera capture controls (shutter button, etc.) when not being moved.

file_operations.description: Create a DraggableMascot component that renders the generated anime-mascot.dim_256x512.png image from frontend/public/assets/generated/anime-mascot.dim_256x512.png as an absolutely positioned overlay. Implement drag functionality using mouse (onMouseDown/onMouseMove/onMouseUp) and touch events (onTouchStart/onTouchMove/onTouchEnd). Track position state (x, y) and dragging state. Apply boundary checks to ensure the mascot stays within the viewport (use window.innerWidth/innerHeight and the mascot's dimensions). Use pointer-events CSS to allow click-through when not dragging. Size the mascot appropriately for mobile (e.g., max-width 80px). Accept optional position and visibility props for integration with REQ-3 animation.

Import and render the DraggableMascot component as an overlay within CameraView. Position it above the camera preview but below any modal overlays (NotPlantOverlay, NotRealOverlay, PlantResultPanel). Ensure the mascot is visible as soon as the camera opens. Use z-index layering to keep the mascot above the video element but not blocking the shutter button. Pass any necessary props for mascot state management.

requirements.summary: Trigger animated sequence where mascot stands beside a board displaying plant benefits with text-to-speech
acceptanceCriteria: After a plant is successfully identified, a board element appears on screen alongside the anime character.
The anime character is positioned standing beside the board.
The plant's beneficial uses are displayed on the board with a typewriter/writing animation.
The Web Speech API reads the beneficial uses text aloud automatically when the board appears.
If SpeechSynthesis is not supported by the browser, the text on the board is still shown silently.
The animation and speech play without blocking the rest of the result UI (plant name, confidence, points).
The board and character can be dismissed or fade out after the speech completes or after a user tap.

file_operations.description: Create a PlantInfoBoard component that displays the generated plant-board.dim_400x300.png image from frontend/public/assets/generated/plant-board.dim_400x300.png with plant benefits text overlaid. Accept props: benefitsText (string), onComplete (callback). Render the board image and animate the text using a typewriter effect (reveal characters incrementally with setTimeout or CSS animation). Use the Web Speech API (window.speechSynthesis) to speak the benefits text when the board appears. Check for SpeechSynthesis support and gracefully degrade if unavailable. Provide a dismiss button or auto-dismiss after speech completes. Style the text to appear chalk-like on the board surface. Position the board to leave space for the mascot beside it.

Import and conditionally render the PlantInfoBoard component within PlantResultPanel after a successful plant identification. Extract the plant's benefits text from the plantData (use the 'benefits' field). Render PlantInfoBoard below or alongside the existing result content. Coordinate the mascot position via a prop or state update to DraggableMascot so it moves beside the board during the animation. Trigger the board appearance with a slight delay after the panel slides up for a staged animation effect. On board completion or dismissal, allow the user to continue interacting with the result panel normally.

Extend DraggableMascot to accept an animationMode prop (e.g., 'free' or 'standBeside'). When animationMode is 'standBeside', animate the mascot to a fixed position next to the board (disable dragging during this mode). Use CSS transitions for smooth movement. Restore draggable behavior when the board is dismissed.

Create a custom hook useSpeechSynthesis that wraps the Web Speech API. Export a speak function that accepts text and optional voice settings. Handle browser compatibility checks (window.speechSynthesis). Return loading/speaking state and an onComplete callback trigger. Use this hook in PlantInfoBoard to manage TTS playback.

requirements.summary: Wire generated anime chibi character illustration into the app as the mascot overlay
acceptanceCriteria: An anime chibi character image is available as a static asset in frontend/public/assets/generated.
The character has a transparent background (PNG).
The character is used in REQ-2 and REQ-3 as the mascot overlay.

file_operations.description: Reference the generated anime-mascot.dim_256x512.png image from the workspace path frontend/public/assets/generated/anime-mascot.dim_256x512.png in the DraggableMascot component's img src attribute. Use the exact generated filename with dimensions. Ensure the image is loaded as a static asset (e.g., src='/assets/generated/anime-mascot.dim_256x512.png'). Apply appropriate width/height or object-fit styling to maintain transparency and aspect ratio.

requirements.summary: Wire generated wooden chalkboard illustration into the app for plant info display
acceptanceCriteria: A chalkboard/wooden board image is available as a static asset in frontend/public/assets/generated.
The board has a transparent or neutral background so it can be overlaid on the camera view.
The board image is used in REQ-3 for the plant info display.

file_operations.description: Reference the generated plant-board.dim_400x300.png image from the workspace path frontend/public/assets/generated/plant-board.dim_400x300.png in the PlantInfoBoard component's img src attribute. Use the exact generated filename with dimensions. Ensure the image is loaded as a static asset (e.g., src='/assets/generated/plant-board.dim_400x300.png'). Position the benefits text as an absolutely positioned overlay on top of the board image to simulate writing on a chalkboard.
